skill.name: rest-patterns
skill.description: Reference guide for REST API design, HTTP methods, status codes, caching headers, rate limiting, response formats, and endpoint conventions.
skill.summary: Design REST APIs with clear HTTP semantics, caching, rate limits, and response patterns.
skill.author: 0xDarkMatter

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found Markdown reference material rather than executable skill code. The external command, weak cryptography, reconnaissance, and network matches are false positives or illustrative examples. Low residual risk remains because users may copy sample curl or fetch snippets against their own endpoints.

low_findings.title: False Positive External Command Flags in Markdown Examples
low_findings.description: The flagged external command patterns are REST endpoint examples, HTTP transcript blocks, or illustrative curl commands. They are documentation content and are not executed by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are Markdown code fences and tables, not runnable scripts or tool invocations. The skill only declares Read and Write tools and contains no executable files.

low_findings.title: False Positive Network Flags in Example URLs and Fetch Code
low_findings.description: The network matches are sample CDN purge URLs, documentation URLs, and a generic retry example. No evidence found of automatic outbound requests or data exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs use example domains or user-supplied parameters inside documentation. There is no active code path that sends secrets or contacts a remote service.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Cryptography and Reconnaissance Flags
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and reconnaissance matches occur in REST terminology, titles, status code guidance, and sample request paths. No cryptographic implementation or system probing behavior is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain documentation headings, HTTP guidance, and example error fields. I did not find evidence of MD5, SHA1, host enumeration, or similar risky implementation behavior.

content.user_title: Design REST APIs With Clear HTTP Patterns
content.value_statement: Teams often make inconsistent choices for methods, status codes, caching, and rate limits. This skill gives concise REST patterns that help Claude, Codex, and Claude Code produce consistent API designs.
actual_capabilities: Maps common CRUD actions to REST endpoints and HTTP methods.
Explains when to use common 2xx, 3xx, 4xx, and 5xx status codes.
Provides caching patterns for Cache-Control, ETag, validation, and CDN behavior.
Summarizes rate limiting strategies, headers, retry behavior, and client backoff.
Shows response format patterns for errors, versioning, bulk operations, and links.
limitations: It is a reference guide, not a live API analyzer.
It does not generate complete framework-specific server implementations.
Examples use placeholder endpoints and need adaptation before production use.
It does not replace current review of RFCs or internal platform standards.

use_cases.title: Plan New API Endpoints
use_cases.description: Choose methods, resource paths, pagination, filtering, and status codes before implementation starts.

use_cases.title: Standardize API Reviews
use_cases.description: Check pull requests for consistent HTTP semantics, caching choices, and error response patterns.

use_cases.title: Document Client Behavior
use_cases.description: Describe retry rules, rate limit headers, cache validation, and response formats for consumers.

prompt_templates.title: Choose Basic Endpoints
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the REST patterns skill to propose endpoints for a user profile resource. Include methods, paths, and expected success status codes.

prompt_templates.title: Review Status Codes
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the REST patterns skill to review these planned API responses. Identify incorrect status codes and suggest better choices with reasons.

prompt_templates.title: Design Caching and Limits
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced performance and abuse protection planning.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the REST patterns skill to define caching headers and rate limit behavior for public list endpoints and authenticated detail endpoints.

prompt_templates.title: Create an API Consistency Checklist
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the REST patterns skill to create a concise review checklist for endpoints, errors, versioning, caching, rate limits, and bulk operations.

output_examples.input: Design endpoints for projects and project members.
output_examples.output: A concise endpoint plan with collection routes, member routes, expected status codes, pagination rules, and validation error handling.

output_examples.input: Review whether an API should return 400, 409, or 422.
output_examples.output: A decision guide explaining syntax errors, state conflicts, and validation failures with recommended response behavior.

output_examples.input: Define caching for public product data and private account data.
output_examples.output: A header strategy that separates shared cacheable responses from private or sensitive responses that should not be stored.

best_practices: Start with resource nouns, then choose methods and status codes from the guide.
Document caching, rate limits, and error formats before clients integrate.
Adapt examples to your authorization model, data sensitivity, and platform standards.
anti_patterns: Do not use verbs in endpoint paths when a resource noun is clearer.
Do not return success codes for validation, authorization, or rate limit failures.
Do not copy example URLs or request snippets into production without review.

faq.question: Does this skill make network requests?
faq.answer: No. It is Markdown reference content. Some examples show URLs or fetch logic for educational purposes.

faq.question: Can it design a full REST API?
faq.answer: It can guide endpoint structure, status codes, headers, and response formats. Implementation details still need engineering review.

faq.question: Does it cover GraphQL or gRPC?
faq.answer: No. The content focuses on REST-style HTTP APIs and related response, caching, and rate limit patterns.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude Code or Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Are the examples production ready?
faq.answer: No. They are patterns and placeholders. Review them against security, privacy, and platform requirements.

faq.question: What security topics are included?
faq.answer: It mentions TLS, authentication, input validation, CORS, rate limits, sensitive URLs, and no-store caching.
